source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Discharge of Synthetic Based Mud Overboard
actions_taken: MPD system put into Flow Control mode
MPD pressure control valves shut by MPD operator
Well shut in by driller using BOP upper annular
Kick circulated out using Drillers Method
Drilling fluid returns examined by Mud Engineer
event.summary: On November 16, 2022, at 05:11 AM, a saltwater kick occurred during drilling of the 12-1/4 inch hole section at approximately 25,000 feet on the Diamond Offshore West Vela drillship. The kick caused Surface Back Pressure in the MPD system to exceed the 1,300 psi PRV setting. Approximately twelve barrels of Synthetic Based Mud were discharged overboard via the MPD system Pressure Relief Valves. The well was successfully shut in and the kick was circulated out. The incident was attributed to human performance error in the decision to divert drilling fluid overboard rather than to the flowline as specified in the New Technology Application.
recommendations: MPD procedures and hole section influx management envelope matrix should include a differential flow threshold to inform the Driller how to respond when using MPD

When using PCVs for MPD, ensure modeling is performed to determine optimal PCV setpoints with down hole pumps on based on maximum kick intensity and maximum PCV setpoint based on required SBP for connections with down hole pumps off
Set maximum PCV pressure to protect against PRVs from opening based on a maximum flow rate for the selected setpoint
Review FMCA for plugging downstream of the MPD choke to confirm the correct PRV set point should limit system to pressure rating of equipment upstream of the choke
Review the latest version of the flow control software that responds to influxes in step changes and switches the MPD from Flow Control to SBP mode when the high-pressure limit is reached

hazards.name: Synthetic Based Mud discharge to environment

threats.name: Saltwater kick below base of salt
Surface Back Pressure exceeds PRV setpoint

consequences.name: Discharge of approximately twelve barrels of SBM to Gulf of Mexico
Potential gas entry into MPD system

controls.name: Managed Pressure Drilling (MPD) System
controls.lod_basis: Active pressure management during drilling operations

supporting_text: First, the MPD system was put into Flow Control mode
the Surface Back Pressure (SBP) in the MPD system increased to over 1,300 psi exceeding the 1,300 psi PRV setting
when the PRV's set point is exceeded the MPD system reverts to 'Manual Mode'

controls.name: Pressure Relief Valves (PRV) at 1,300 psi setpoint
controls.lod_basis: Designed to relieve pressure when system exceeds setpoint

supporting_text: the Surface Back Pressure (SBP) in the MPD system increased to over 1,300 psi exceeding the 1,300 psi PRV setting for the MPD system
The Maximum allowable SBP according to the MPD plan was 1,300 psi. MPD PRV's were set at 1,300 psi

controls.name: Blowout Preventor (BOP) upper annular
controls.lod_basis: Secondary barrier to isolate well when primary controls fail

supporting_text: the well was shut in by the driller using the Blowout Preventor's (BOP) upper annular
At 05:20 AM the well was completely shut in and the shut-in drill pipe pressure was 1,600 psi

controls.name: MPD diverter valve configuration
controls.barrier_role: Containment routing for pressure relief discharge
controls.lod_basis: Directs discharge to safe location to prevent environmental release

supporting_text: The MPD diverter valve was lined up to go overboard as opposed to the flowline as shown in the MPD new technology drilling application
The Drilling Supervisor stated in his interview they sent the SBM overboard to avoid gas entering MPD system
Appendix G.1.5 of the New Technology Application Diamond West Vela East Breaks 699 well 001 rev. 01, shows the flow path going to the flowline during a PRV release
Human performance error - Decision to divert the drilling fluid overboard

controls.name: Well control procedures and MPD plan
controls.lod_basis: Procedures define response to influx events

supporting_text: the drilling crew responded appropriately to the incident and shut in the well as directed by the onsite well control procedures and according to the MPD plan

The MPD system is not designed to control any kick or influx greater than five barrels. According to the Hoodoo EB 699 MPD Plan. The MPD plan for the well states any influx greater than 5 barrels must be circulated out through the choke manifold

controls.name: Mud Engineer examination of drilling fluid returns
controls.lod_basis: Identifies influx type to inform response strategy

supporting_text: Drilling fluid returns were examined by the Mud Engineer, and the evidence indicated a saltwater kick without hydrocarbons

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
